name: MAP Over Combinator
description: Slice input into windows or parameter sets, apply a body subgraph to each slice independently, and aggregate the per-slice results into a combined output.
dejargonized_summary: Break the input into pieces, process each piece the same way, and combine all the piece-level results into one answer.

inputs.name: data
inputs.constraints: Sliceable input. Can be a signal (windowed by position), a parameter list (iterated per value), or a collection (processed per element).

outputs.name: combined_results
outputs.constraints: Aggregated output from all slices. Shape depends on the collect strategy (stack, average, concatenate, etc.).

planning_constraints.subject: slice_independence
planning_constraints.statement: Each slice must be processable independently of other slices.
planning_constraints.rationale: MAP semantics require that body_process has no cross-slice state dependencies. If slices depend on each other, use FIXED_POINT or SEQUENTIAL_FILTER instead.

planning_constraints.subject: collect_strategy
planning_constraints.statement: The collect_results stage must define how per-slice outputs are combined.
planning_constraints.rationale: Common strategies: weighted average (threshold ensembles), concatenation (windowed transforms), max/voting (detection sweeps). The choice affects downstream semantics.

stages.name: Window Slicer
stages.description: Produce overlapping windows, parameter grid entries, or element-wise slices from the input data.
stages.dejargonized_description: Break the input into the pieces that will each be processed separately.

outputs.name: window
outputs.constraints: A single slice for body processing. Iterated over all slices.

preconditions: Input data is available and has a sliceable structure.
guarantees: Each output window is a well-formed independent slice of the input.

stages.name: Body Init
stages.description: Initialize per-window processing state. May be trivial (pass-through) or set up local context.
stages.dejargonized_description: Set up any local state needed before processing each piece.

inputs.name: window
inputs.constraints: A single slice from the slicer.

guarantees: State is initialized for body processing.

stages.name: Body Process
stages.description: Process a single window or parameter configuration. This is the stage the architect decomposes further — it represents the per-slice computation that is applied identically to every slice.
stages.dejargonized_description: Do the actual work on each piece. This is where the real computation happens.
guarantees: Output is independent of other windows.
family_notes: This stage is abstract — the architect must decompose it into concrete atoms based on the problem domain.
Example: for a threshold sweep ensemble, body_process is the full preprocessing + model + scoring sub-pipeline.

stages.name: Collect Results
stages.description: Aggregate per-window results into the final combined output. Strategy depends on the problem: weighted average for ensembles, concatenation for transforms, voting for classification.
stages.dejargonized_description: Combine all the piece-level results into one final answer.
guarantees: Output combines all per-window results according to the chosen strategy.

audit.rationale: MAP_OVER combinator pattern surfaced as a systematic gap in solution CDG analysis: both the Connectomics threshold sweep ensemble (120 thresholds x 4 filters) and Cause-Effect asymmetric feature difference (compute both directions, subtract) require this pattern.
audit.dejargonized_summary: Break the input into pieces, process each piece the same way, and combine the results. Used for parameter sweeps, sliding windows, and per-element processing.
provenance_notes: This JSON asset replaces the Python-only definition with an auditable, versioned asset.
uncertainty_notes: The body_process stage is deliberately abstract (concept_type=custom) — the architect must decompose it per-problem.
The collect_results strategy (average vs concat vs vote) is not constrained by this skeleton; the architect must choose.

references.title: Solution CDG Gap Analysis
references.note: S-2 orchestration gap: threshold_sweep_ensemble and asymmetric_feature_difference require MAP_OVER.

references.title: MAP combinator pattern
references.note: Standard higher-order function semantics for independent per-element processing.
